title: Lithium Acetate / SDS Extraction of Genomic DNA from Saccharomyces cerevisiae
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ehuo9","text":"This protocol is just a quick description of the protocol provided by Looke et al., 2011 (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3182553/). The purpose of this protocol is simply to provide a quick and easy method to isolate genomic DNA from yeast for PCR-based applications. While it does not provide the purist genomic DNA that you can get, it is extremely quick and easy to perform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":90,"length":53}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"02E6F803CA5B11EBA9BE0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3182553"}}}}
steps: Pick one yeast colony from the plate or spin down 100-200 μl of liquid yeast culture (OD600=0.4). Suspend cells in 100 μl of 200mM LiOAc, 1 % SDS solution. Alternatively, you can spin down 100 - 500 uL of liquid culture at 3,000 rcf for 4 minutes and then resuspend the pelleted cells in 100 uL of 200 mM LiOAc, 1% SDS solution if you wish.
Spin down DNA and cell debris at 15,000 rcf for 3 minutes.
Resuspend the pellet in 70% ethanol. Then spin the resuspended DNA and cell debris mixture at 15,000 rcf for 3 minutes.
Dispose of the supernatant and then dissolve the pellet in 100 μl of H2O or TE and spin down cell debris for 15 seconds at 15 000 g.
Use 1 μl of supernatant for PCR.
creator: Clark Fritsch
